eyeResults.description: Per-lane eye height/width at the compliance test point so a gate review can cite the SI sign-off structurally.
eyeResults.schema: array<{ lane: string, corner: string, eyeHeightMv: number, eyeWidthPs: number, pass: boolean }>

criteria.criterion: PASS if DDR5 eye height and eye width are compared against the ≥100 mV / ≥50 ps hard gate at the designated test point (TP3). FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when the gate is misstated or the comparison is wrong.

criteria.criterion: PASS if the eye is evaluated at the −40 °C worst-case cold corner (the bounding case for setup-time margin), not only nominal. FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when the bounding corner is omitted.

criteria.criterion: PASS if the hard-gate eye is demonstrated with receiver DFE/CTLE and transmitter de-emphasis/pre-shoot disabled, proving raw-channel compliance. FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when the passing result relies on equalization.

criteria.criterion: PASS if the analysis is run at ≥6400 MT/s (or the higher rated data rate with proportionally scaled eye requirement). FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when run below the minimum rate.

criteria.criterion: PASS if differential trace impedance is verified against 85 Ω ±10% via field-solver extraction and confirmed by TDR, with excursions treated as non-conformances. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if both pre-layout (schematic-level) and post-layout (3D-extracted) simulations are performed and hard gates re-verified on the extracted model. FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when only one stage is present.

criteria.criterion: PASS if the eye is swept across the required process/voltage/temperature corners, not a single operating point. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if NEXT/FEXT crosstalk-induced jitter is quantified and included in the composite eye at TP3. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if per-lane results are reported and the worst-case lane is identified (e.g. DQ60 cold-corner). FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when only an aggregate eye is given.

criteria.criterion: PASS if any pre/post discrepancy exceeding 10% of the threshold carries a correlation note, and any failing lane carries a documented corrective action (fanout, via-stub, or stackup). FAIL otherwise.
PASS if the report cites the Signal Integrity Analysis Sign-Off SOP (SUM-SI-SOP-022) as the source of the eye hard gates. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if reported eye numbers cite the underlying measurement/simulation data (e.g. SUM-RAI-SI-001a). FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when eye values are asserted with no data source.

criteria.criterion: PASS if DDR5 electrical requirements are attributed to the JEDEC JESD79-5 reference summary (SUM-STD-JES79-5). FAIL otherwise.
PASS if impedance/stackup claims reference the PCB Stackup Optimization Guide (SUM-STD-INT-PCB-001). FAIL otherwise.
PASS if the 100 mV/50 ps thresholds trace to the Threshold Registry (SUM-REG-THR-001) and any SI risk closed (e.g. R-004) cites the risk register. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if every regulatory clause, standard, SOP, procedure, and controlled-document identifier cited in the output appears in the retrieved sources. FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when any citation is invented or cannot be traced.

criteria.criterion: PASS if every measurement, margin, count, score, and figure in the output traces to the retrieved data, tables, or tool results. FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when any number is unsupported.

criteria.criterion: PASS if the output contains every section the governing procedure requires for a signal integrity report. FAIL otherwise.
criteria.explanation: Fails when a required section is missing or empty.

criteria.criterion: PASS if every factual claim in the output is supported by the retrieved sources and the exact data provided. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if the output is written clearly and unambiguously, appropriate for a controlled quality record, with correct document and revision identifiers. FAIL otherwise.

trajectory.description: Provenance checks against the recorded retrieval trajectory for a signal-integrity report.

required.reason: The eye-diagram hard gates (≥100 mV / ≥50 ps) are defined in SUM-SI-SOP-022; an SI report without it has no authority for its pass/fail claim.
The eye thresholds are registered in SUM-REG-THR-001 and must be traced there structurally.
